channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Argentine Organisation Rehomes Laboratory Rats and Mice Through Annual Event
item.description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, an annual event called Ratapalooza brings attention to the adoption of laboratory rats and mice that are no longer needed for scientific research. Organised by Team Ratas, ...

item.title: Dennis Hopper: Actor, Director, and Photographer of the 1960s
item.description: Dennis Hopper (1936–2010) was a defining figure of the 1960s, known not only for his acting but also for his work behind the camera as a director and photographer. After early career setbacks and a br...

item.title: USM Alger Wins CAF Confederation Cup After Penalty Shootout Against Zamalek SC
item.description: USM Alger claimed their second TotalEnergies CAF Confederation Cup title after an intense final against Zamalek SC that ended in an 8-7 penalty shootout victory. The two-legged final concluded with a ...

item.title: Federal authorities' response and inquiry process following Bondi terror attack detailed in documents
item.description: Documents obtained under Freedom of Information laws reveal the frantic internal efforts within the Prime Minister’s department immediately following the deadly Bondi terror attack on December 14. Aut...

item.title: Senator Bala Na’Allah Withdraws from Kebbi South APC Senatorial Primaries Over Alleged Pre-Determined Outcome
item.description: Former Deputy Senate Leader, Senator Bala Ibn Na’Allah, don withdraw him self from the All Progressives Congress (APC) primaries for Kebbi South Senatorial District wey suppose hold on May 18, 2026. F...

item.title: Historic Westminster Hotel in Dawson City Destroyed by Fire
item.description: On Sunday morning, Dawson City, Yukon, witnessed the destruction of the Westminster Hotel, affectionately known as 'The Pit,' after a fire engulfed the historic building. Dating back to 1898, the hote...

item.title: The Journey of a 17th-Century Mughal Carpet to London and Its Historical Significance
item.description: The Girdlers’ carpet, crafted in the 17th century during the Mughal Empire, represents a remarkable story of craftsmanship, trade, and cultural exchange. Commissioned by Robert Bell, a director of the...

item.title: Seven-Day Fasting Study Shows Molecular Changes Across the Human Body
item.description: A new study from Queen Mary University of London and the Norwegian School of Sports Sciences investigated how the human body responds to seven days of water-only fasting. Researchers monitored 12 heal...

item.title: Dutch Prime Minister Discusses 2016 Child Abduction Case with India
item.description: During his official visit to India, Dutch Prime Minister Rob Jetten raised concerns regarding the 2016 abduction of a young girl named Insiya. The child, who was two years old at the time, was alleged...

item.title: Mitchell Starc takes three wickets in a standout IPL performance
item.description: Australian fast bowler Mitchell Starc delivered an impressive performance in the Indian Premier League, claiming three wickets in a single over. This standout display demonstrated his skill and precis...

item.title: Marilyn Monroe’s Costume and Hair Preparations for Niagara (1953)
item.description: Niagara (1953) was a landmark film for Marilyn Monroe, showcasing her as a glamorous and seductive figure in a Technicolor noir thriller directed by Henry Hathaway. The studio, 20th Century Fox, inves...

item.title: Aaron Rai wins PGA Championship with strong final round at Aronimink
item.description: England’s Aaron Rai claimed his first major title by winning the PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club on Sunday. The 31-year-old produced a brilliant final round of 65 to finish at nine under par w...

item.title: Navy Jets Collide Midair at Idaho Airshow, Pilots Eject Safely
item.description: During the Gunfighter Skies airshow near Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho, a dramatic midair collision occurred involving two Boeing EA-18G Growlers. The incident forced all four pilots to eject ...

item.title: How the summit highlighted the need to rethink safety in Nairobi
item.description: The article argues that the recent summit in Nairobi should be seen not only as a diplomatic or event-management exercise, but as a chance to rethink what a truly safe and liveable city could look lik...

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda as International Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda as a public health emergency of international concern. The announcem...

item.title: Profile of Sophie Brussaux: French Artist, Mother of Adonis Graham
item.description: Sophie Brussaux is a 37-year-old French visual artist, philanthropist, and former model and adult entertainer. Born on 1 August 1989 in Bordeaux, France, she is of white French ethnicity and comes fro...

item.title: Man Arrested in Tustin for Shooting Ex-Girlfriend and Fleeing to Mexico
item.description: Tustin police arrested 47-year-old Juan Marquez in connection with the fatal shooting of his ex-girlfriend, 42-year-old Sandra Fernandez. The incident occurred on Thursday near the intersection of Yor...

item.title: Questions raised over PM Modi’s roadshows amid call for public austerity
item.description: On May 10, Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s motorcade travelled through Jamnagar, Gujarat, just hours after he urged citizens to adopt austerity measures to cope with economic challenges arising from th...

item.title: The Impact of Diagnostic Gaps on Mental Health in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, mental health often fails not quietly, but structurally. Studies and WHO estimates suggest that between 75% and 90% of people with mental health conditions never receive treatment, le...

item.title: Carol Banawa mourns the passing of her mother after cancer battle
item.description: Filipino singer and actress Carol Banawa is grieving the loss of her mother, Cirila Banawa, who passed away after a courageous battle with cancer. The family confirmed the sad news on May 18, 2026. Ca...
